sourcesContent:
# multiline collection layout : newlines
# inside brackets separate elements, deeper lines open INDENT blocks,
# closers auto-close them; lines ending in an unfinished operator or
# starting with a continuer (',', '.', '?.') extend the logical line.
mk = (o) -> o
add = (a, b) -> a + b
pair = (a, b) -> [a, b]

# the headline forms: multiline object, array, and call
config = {
  name: "app"
  port: 8080
}
list = [
  1
  2
  3
]
sum = add(
  1
  2
)

# mixed separators and trailing commas
mixed = [
  1, 2, 3
  4, 5
]
trail = {
  a: 1,
  b: 2,
}

# nesting: arrays of arrays, objects in arrays, collections in objects
grid = [
  [1, 2]
  [3, 4]
]
recs = [
  {a: 1}
  {b: 2}
]
data = {
  list: [
    1
    2
  ]
  fn: mk(
    3
  )
}

# blank and comment lines inside brackets are trivia
holey = [
  1

  # a comment between elements
  2
]

# an unindented OBJECT body parses (the empty pair-list row admits it)
flat = {
a: 1
b: 2
}

# a closer on the last element's line
tight = [
  7
  8]

# implicit-call continuation across a trailing comma; indented-object
# call arguments; a second object argument after a comma
sum2 = add 1,
  2
made = mk
  a: 1
  b: 2
two = pair {a: 1},
  {b: 2}

# leading-dot chain lines close the implicit call and chain on it
chain = mk name: "x"
.name
count = list
  .filter (n) -> n > 1
  .length

# unfinished operators at line ends continue the logical line
total = 1 +
  2 +
  3

# multiline parentheticals and parameter lists
avg = (
  1 + 2
)
join = (
  a
  b
) -> a + b
def prod(
  x
  y
)
  x * y

# indented-object bodies for return; multiline index and super-index
build = ->
  return
    a: 1
    b: 2
class Box
  constructor: (v) ->
    @v = v
  get: (k) ->
    @v[
      k
    ]
class SubBox extends Box
  get: (k) ->
    m = "get"
    super[
      m
    ](k) + 1

# comprehension over a multiline array
doubled = (n * 2 for n in [
  10
  20
])

results = [
  config.port
  list.length
  sum
  mixed.length
  trail.b
  grid[1][0]
  recs[0].a
  data.list[1]
  data.fn
  holey[1]
  flat.b
  tight[1]
  sum2
  made.b
  two[1].b
  chain
  count
  total
  avg
  join(2, 3)
  prod(4, 5)
  build().a
  new SubBox({k: 9}).get("k")
  doubled[1]
]
